intro: Converting from TypeScript to Python shifts a project from a client/server ecosystem bound to web platforms to an indentation-driven scripting ecosystem dominating data engineering, ML, and backend APIs. Developers execute this switch to leverage Python's extensive data science ecosystems and clean, readable design paradigms. This changes the design pattern from compile-time type erasure to structural type verification handled during interpretation or static analysis via type hints.

diffs.from: Statically checked structurally at compile time; types are completely erased prior to runtime execution.
diffs.to: Dynamically checked at runtime with structural or nominal static type hints validated via external checkers like mypy.

diffs.from: C-style syntax containing explicit brace boundaries, semicolons, and arrow parameters.
diffs.to: Clean block structure relying strictly on indentation depths, colons, and keyword parameter operators.

diffs.from: Engine-driven garbage collection running within a single-threaded runtime environment.
diffs.to: Reference counting combined with a cyclical garbage collector, bounded by the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL).

diffs.from: Event-loop baseline using native Promise classes and built-in async/await syntax markers.
diffs.to: Cooperative multitasking powered by an event loop infrastructure exposed via the `asyncio` standard library package.

diffs.from: EcmaScript foundation focusing on basic primitives; heavily reliant on third-party npm wrappers.
diffs.to: Batteries-included toolkit offering deep operating system bindings, parsing tools, and mathematical modules natively.

examples.title: Type-Hinted Function Contracts
examples.description: Defining structured parameters along with distinct object signatures.

examples.title: List Comprehensive Filtering
examples.description: Extracting matching values from collection vectors based on attributes.
examples.fromCode:
const nums: number[] = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
const evens: number[] = nums.filter(x => x % 2 === 0);
examples.toCode:
nums: list[int] = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
evens: list[int] = [x for x in nums if x % 2 == 0]

tips.title: Leverage External Type Checkers
tips.body: Python ignores type hints at runtime and during interpretation. To replicate TypeScript's strict compile-time validation errors, integrate `mypy` or `pyright` into your CI/CD pipelines.

tips.title: Replace Array Methods with Comprehensions
tips.body: Instead of mapping and filtering Python lists via continuous chained methods like `.filter()` or `.map()`, utilize idiomatic list comprehensions, which compile to faster loop bytecodes.

tips.title: Account for the GIL in Async Code
tips.body: Async functions in Python prevent blocking operations on the primary thread but do not run code in parallel across CPU cores. For compute-bound routines, delegate workloads to the `multiprocessing` library.

tips.title: Map Dictionary Semantics Explicitly
tips.body: TypeScript object literals act as both maps and structural objects. In Python, distinguish between structured class models (`dataclasses`) and dynamic key-value storage dictionary payloads (`dict`).
